summary: Iteration 17: Complete E2E testing of Bulk Upload functionality. Fixed route ordering bug in backend (process-now route was being matched by {upload_type} route). All 13 backend tests pass, all frontend tests pass.

backend: GET /api/bulk-upload/status returns pending/processed for all 3 types (naukri, pipeline, score)
POST /api/bulk-upload/naukri accepts CSV file and saves to pending
Bulk upload endpoints require authentication (401 without auth)
frontend: Dashboard shows 3 Bulk buttons (bulk-naukri-btn, bulk-pipeline-btn, bulk-score-btn)
Clicking bulk-naukri-btn opens BulkUploadModal with title 'Bulk Upload — Naukri Applies'
Closing modal via X button works
Clicking bulk-pipeline-btn opens modal with 'Pipeline' in title
Closing modal via clicking overlay works
Clicking bulk-score-btn opens modal with 'Score Sheet' in title
Navigation button Summary (nav-summary-btn) works - navigates to /summary
Navigation button Applicants (nav-roles-btn) works - navigates to /roles
Navigation button Attended (nav-attended-btn) works - navigates to /attended-roles
Single upload buttons present: upload-naukri-btn, upload-pipeline-btn, upload-scoresheet-btn

bug_fixed.root_cause: Route ordering issue - /api/bulk-upload/{upload_type} was defined before /api/bulk-upload/process-now, so FastAPI matched 'process-now' as an upload_type
bug_fixed.fix: Moved /api/bulk-upload/process-now route definition before /api/bulk-upload/{upload_type} in server.py

seed_data_creation: Test CSV files created during testing for bulk upload verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bulk upload functionality fully tested and working. The app now has: (1) Dashboard with 3 Bulk buttons next to each upload type, (2) BulkUploadModal component with file upload, pending list, processed list, delete, and process-now functionality, (3) Background processor runs every 30s automatically. Upload directories are at /app/uploads/{naukri,pipeline,score}/{pending,processed}. Route ordering fix applied - specific routes must come before parameterized routes in FastAPI.
